title: glaucium grandiflorum var. iranicum
intro: Glaucium grandiflorum var. iranicum, also known as the Iranian poppy or horn poppy, is a perennial flowering plant native to Iran and other parts of the Middle East. It is a species of Glaucium, a genus of flowering plants in the poppy family (Papaveraceae), characterized by its vibrant orange-red flowers and medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat a range of health issues, including respiratory problems, fever, and rheumatism, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's dried roots and flowers are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and other respiratory conditions.
